People: 1700: Wn Wilson (OSea), whilst laying down from aloft fell from the topmast rigging striking the shear ratline iron, of the port lower rigging and then struck the water; The following men jumped overboard and did their utmost to save him. [Names listed].. Commenced to drag, and sent Native diver down who found the body but failed to bring it to the surface, after dragging sometime recovered the body and brought it on board - finding that the skull had been fractured.

1430: Rev Mr McIntosh came on board: Called all hands bury the dead: Performed a portion of the burial service, and sent 8 marines and 51 marinirs, with 8 Commissioned and Warrant Officers as funeral procession to accompany the body of William Wilson (O Sea), to the grave.. 1700 funeral party returned to ship.

Other: Noon: Sent on board HBMS !?? for transportation to the United States a box containing specimans obtained from sounding from San Francisco to Hololulu, to go from san Francisco to Washington in care of Wells Fargo express:

Ships: 0100: The English Mail Steamer !City of Melbourne! came in from Australia.  %^)

People: 1115: The Prince {Leleiohoku} visited the !Portsmouth!. All the men of war in the harbor manning the yards and the !Portsmouth! firing a  salute of 21 guns with the Hawian Royal standard on the main.

Note: 1532: Got Underway. Towing Portsmouth out of the harbor.
1635: Took departure - Diamond Pt brg ENE1/2E 5mi

Other: 0800: Sent 3rd cutter with an officer to blow up a rock in the entrance to the harbor.. 3rd cutter returned to ship and reported having exploded a torpedo at WhaleRock.

People: 1100: Fired a salute of 13 guns in honor of Col. A. B. Steinberger U.S. Commissioner, who was to deliver a sealed letter from the President of the U.S. to the King of Samoa.

Other: 1230: Fired a salute of 21 guns when the newly adopted Samoan Ensign was run up on the point: The natives also fired a salute of 21 guns.

Note: It looks like he switch to the mercury barometer from 0400-1900. I adjusted the baro readings to reflect the aneroid readings.

People: 0010: Andrew Gilliam, SM Mate, was found to be dead having died of heart disease between 11 and 12 o'clock. Brought his body up and laid it on the quarter deck. At 1335 called all hands bury the dead: The funeral service was read over the body of And Gilliam (SM Mate) by the Executive Officer: At 1350 the boat carring the remains and excort shoved off; The escort consisted of 17 men & 5 Officers. At 1455 the party returned.
08-1200: King Putepule came onboard and was presented with a Samoan flag by Comdg Officer.

Note: 1317: Got Underway. Standing out of Hbr. 1330 Stopped for soundings. 1340 Ahead.
1535: Took departure. Center of Apia Hbr brg SSW 8mi.
0640: S1/2W and put back for Apia.

Other: 1135: By order of Comdg Officer the allowance of water was reduced to 1-1/3 Gale for each man.
1100: Allowance of bread was reduced one half.

Note: 1232: Anchored. Hololulu HI Noon: Hololulu HI
1700: Took departure Makapun Pt bore SSW1/2W 8 mi

People: 2330: A stow away was found on board and reported.
1600: Placed Thos Francis (Sea) in double irons in charge of sentry (for safe keeping) for stabbing with a knife Edwrd McGinty (Sea) taken on board at Hololulu for passage to San Francisco.

Note: 1215: NNE Noon: Enroute San Francisco Hbr
For these 12 hours, the log keeper wrote 30.00 followed by dittoes. I changed the reading to 30.30.
I don't know what to do with the baro readings for the next 36 hours. They are just 30.00 with decimals. Left them as is, but they look wrong.
0800: Sent to Navy yard Chronometer No 2519 (Dait) and received in exchange chronometer No 1451 (Negus)
1745: Anchored. Nr PMSSCo wharf in Mission bay
1210: Set course SWxS Noon: Leaving San Francisco Harbor.
1445: Took departure Farallon Ids brg NW3/4N 12mi, course SSW
Noon: WxS . Set clocks back 6 min.

Other: 0355: A  meteor of extraordinary brilliance was observed, its course being from the zenith towards the East.

Note: 0700: NW end of Maui S3/4W, Eastern end of Molokai SW1/2S
1400: SW1/2W, 1435 sighted Oahu on stbd bow
0000: Steaming into Hololulu 2400: Hololulu light bore NxW3/4W

Ships: 1120: Peterel put to sea bound for Pittcairns Island.  {Pitcairn island}

Note: His next 12 baro readings are suspicious. They jump up 0.10 and then down 0.12 inches.
1100: Stopped. Sounding 1126: Got Underway. Course SSW
1257: Took departure Diamond Hd bore N401/2E, Waimaue N36W.

Other: 2000: Observed a brilliant appearance of the Zodiacal lights
1115: Sounding, reached bottom in 3080 fathoms. At 1120 commenced reeling in: sent down on wire a bottle of Champagne which came up unimpaired.

Note: 1215: SW Heading for Northern end of Enderbury Id
1600: Hulle (?) Id bore S55W to S80W
